steps.title: Identify the decisive analogy
steps.content: The passage compares historical knowledge to a map—factually accurate but “cold and naked”—versus landscape paintings with “mimic charm.” The contrast is clear: we receive facts, not vivid personality.

steps.title: Apply the analogy to the choices
steps.content: Choice C captures this exactly: historical figures are documented as “a collection of facts” rather than as “a representation of their personalities”—the map versus painting analogy in action. The other choices either overlook the fact-versus-personality contrast (A, B) or mistake it for a utility comparison (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
